text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where developers regularly merge their code changes into a central repository. Each merge triggers an automated build process, which compiles the code into a runnable application. Following the build, a suite of automated tests is executed to verify that the new code does not break existing functionality. The main goal of CI is to catch issues early, right after each change is made. This is done through CI pipelines, which define the steps needed to build, test, and validate code. By running tests frequently, teams can identify and fix problems quickly, avoiding major conflicts later.
